subject: Statistics
title: The Greatest Athlete by the Numbers
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Greatest Athlete by the Numbers.

hook.text: Who's the greatest athlete ever? Jordan, Gretzky, Bolt, Phelps? Statistics can try to answer — but can it really?

buildup.text: Standard deviations above the mean in their sport is one way to compare across different fields.

discovery.text: Don Bradman's cricket batting average is 4.4 standard deviations above the mean — the most dominant ever.

twist.text: Comparing across sports requires choosing metrics, and every choice is debatable. The question may have no answer.

climax.text: Statistics can quantify dominance within a sport. Cross-sport comparisons are fascinating but ultimately subjective.

punchline.text: By the numbers, Bradman. By your heart, your favorite. Both are valid.

quiz.question: How can statistics compare athletes across different sports?
options: By measuring how many standard deviations above the mean each athlete performs in their sport
By counting total career wins regardless of sport
